title: Method for improving the taste of moringa seeds while retaining their nutritive value
abstract: A method for treating moringa seeds is disclosed. A starting material comprising ground moringa seeds, preferably seeds that have undergone a process to remove their oil, is contacted with water. The water is drained, and the moringa seeds are then contacted with water a second time. The product may then be dried or passed to an extruder. The process almost completely removes the unpleasant bitter taste associated with moringa seeds, but retains most of the protein content of the starting material. In contrast to methods known in the art, the method does not require adjustment of the pH or the use of any organic solvent. A composition comprising ground moringa seeds that comprises at least 30% protein and has a glucosinolate concentration <10% of that of naturally-occurring moringa seeds, while lacking the bitter taste associated with raw moringa seeds, is also disclosed.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS We claim: 1. A method for treating moringa seeds, wherein said method comprises: obtaining a starting material comprising a material selected from the group consisting of ground moringa seeds, ground germinated moringa seeds, and ground fermented moringa seeds; contacting said starting material with a first quantity of water for a first predetermined period of time, thereby producing a soaked moringa seed material; separating at least partially said soaked moringa seed material from said water; contacting said soaked moringa seed material with a second quantity of water for a second predetermined period of time, thereby producing debittered moringa seed material; and, separating at least partially said debittered moringa seed material from said water. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein said ground moringa seeds comprise ground dehulled moringa seeds. 3. The method according to claim 1, wherein said starting material comprises ground germinated moringa seeds. 4. The method according to claim 1, wherein said starting material comprises ground fermented moringa seeds. 5. The method according to claim 1, wherein said starting material comprises ground moringa seeds that have undergone a process to remove oil at least partially from said moringa seeds. 6. The method according to claim 5, wherein said starting material comprises milled de-oiled moringa seed cake. 7. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said starting material with a first quantity of water comprises: placing said starting material in a vessel; adding an excess of water to said vessel; and, allowing said starting material and said first quantity of water to remain in said vessel for said first predetermined period of time. 8. The method according to claim 7, wherein said step of contacting said starting material with a first quantity of water comprises mixing or stirring said starting material and said water during said first predetermined period of time. 9. The method according to claim 1, wherein said first predetermined period of time is between 30 and 90 minutes. 10. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said starting material with a first quantity of water comprises contacting said starting material with sufficient water to provide a water : starting material weight ratio of between 3 and 20. 11. The method according to claim 10, wherein said step of contacting said starting material with a first quantity of water comprises contacting said starting material with sufficient water to provide a water : starting material weight ratio of about 4:1. 12. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said starting material with a first quantity of water comprises contacting said starting material with water, the pH of which has been adjusted to be less than 7. 13. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said starting material with a first quantity of water comprises contacting said starting material with water, the pH of which has been adjusted to be greater than 7. 14. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said starting material with a first quantity of water comprises contacting said starting material with room-temperature water. 15. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said starting material with a first quantity of water comprises contacting said starting material with water the temperature of which is < 10 Â°C. 16. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said starting material with a first quantity of water comprises contacting said starting material with water the temperature of which is between 85 Â°C and 99 Â°C. 17. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of separating at least partially said soaked moringa seed material from said water is performed by a method selected from the group consisting of filtering, decanting, and centrifuging. 18. The method according to claim 1, wherein said second predetermined period of time is between 5 and 15 minutes. 19. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said soaked moringa seed material with a second quantity of water comprises: placing said soaked moringa seed material in a vessel; adding an excess of water to said vessel; and, allowing said starting material and said first quantity of water to remain in said vessel for said second predetermined period of time. 20. The method according to claim 19, wherein said step of contacting said soaked moringa seed material with said second quantity of water comprises mixing or stirring said soaked moringa seed material and said water during said second predetermined period of time. 21. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said soaked moringa with said second quantity of water comprises rinsing said soaked moringa seed material. 22. The method according to claim 21, wherein said step of rinsing said soaked moringa seed material is preceded by a step of placing said step of placing said soaked moringa seed material on a filter. 23. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said soaked moringa seed material with a second quantity of water comprises contacting said soaked moringa seed material with sufficient water to provide a water : starting material weight ratio of between 3 and 20. 24. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said soaked moringa seed material with a second quantity of water comprises contacting said soaked moringa seed material with sufficient water to provide a water : starting material weight ratio of about 4:1. 25. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said soaked moringa seed material with a second quantity of water comprises contacting said soaked moringa seed material with water, the pH of which has been adjusted to be less than 7. 26. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said soaked moringa seed material with a second quantity of water comprises contacting said soaked moringa seed material with water, the pH of which has been adjusted to be greater than 7. 27. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said soaked moringa seed material with a second quantity of water comprises contacting said soaked moringa seed material with room-temperature water. 28. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said soaked moringa seed material with a second quantity of water comprises contacting said soaked moringa seed material with water the temperature of which is < 10 Â°C. 29. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of contacting said soaked moringa seed material with a second quantity of water comprises contacting said soaked moringa seed material with water the temperature of which is between 85 Â°C and 99 Â°C. 30. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of separating at least partially said debittered moringa seed material from said water is performed by a method selected from the group consisting of filtering, decanting, and centrifuging. 31. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of separating at least partially said debittered moringa seed material from said water is followed by a step of drying said debittered moringa seed material. 32. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of separating at least partially said debittered moringa seed material from said water is followed by a step of passing said debittered moringa seed material to an extruder. 33. The method according to claim 1, wherein said method does not comprise any step requiring use of organic solvent. 34. The method according to claim 1, wherein said method does not comprise any step of adjusting pH. 35. A composition comprising moringa seed material derived from a starting material selected from the group consisting of ground moringa seeds, ground germinated moringa seeds, and ground fermented moringa seeds, wherein said composition comprises at least 30% protein (w/w) and is characterized by a glucomoringin concentration that is less than 30% (w/w) of that of said starting material. 36. The composition according to claim 35, wherein said composition is characterized by a glucomoringin concentration that is less than 5% (w/w) of that of said starting material. 37. The composition according to claim 35, wherein said composition comprises at least 40% protein. 38. The composition according to claim 35, wherein said composition has not been produced by any process involving contact with organic solvent. 39. The composition according to claim 35, wherein said composition is characterized by a bitterness taste value < 2 on a bitterness scale in which 0 represents no bitter taste at all, and 5 represents an extremely bitter taste. 40. The composition according to claim 35, wherein said composition is characterized by a glucosinolate concentration that is less than 30% (w/w) of that of said starting material. 41. The composition according to claim 40, wherein said composition is characterized by a glucosinolate concentration that is less than 5% (w/w) of that of said starting material. 42. The composition according to claim 40, wherein said composition comprises at least 40% protein. 43. The composition according to claim 40, wherein said starting material has not undergone any process involving contact with organic solvent. 44. The composition according to claim 40, wherein said composition is characterized by a bitterness taste value < 2 on a bitterness scale in which 0 represents no bitter taste at all, and 5 represents an extremely bitter taste. 45. The composition according to any one of claims 35 - 44, wherein said composition is characterized by at least one of the following: said composition comprises 30 - 60% (w/w) protein; said composition comprises 20 - 60% (w/w) protein (dry basis); said composition comprises 0.5 - 16% (w/w) fat; said composition comprises 18 - 55% (w/w) fiber; said composition comprises 15 - 40% (w/w) soluble fiber; said composition comprises 0.5 - 3.5% (w/w) sugar; said composition comprises 2 - 3% (w/w) ash; and, said composition is characterized by a loss on drying of 1 - 10% (w/w). 46. The composition according to any one of claims 35 - 44, wherein said composition comprises proteins having molecular weights, as determined by SDS-PAGE electrophoresis with Coomassie staining, of 43.7 Â± 1.3 kDa, 32.9 Â± 1.0 kDa, 30.4 Â± 0.9 kDa, 19.7 Â± 0.5 kDa, and 6 Â±1 kDa. 47. The composition according to any one of claims 35 - 44, wherein said composition comprises proteins having molecular weights identical to within one standard deviation of molecular 

 weights of proteins of said starting material, as determined by SDS-PAGE electrophoresis with Cooomassie staining. 48. The composition according to claim 45, wherein said composition comprises proteins having molecular weights, as determined by SDS-PAGE electrophoresis with Coomassie staining, of 43.7 Â± 1.3 kDa, 32.9 Â± 1.0 kDa, 30.4 Â± 0.9 kDa, 19.7 Â± 0.5 kDa, and 6 Â±1 kDa. 49. The composition according to claim 46, wherein said composition comprises proteins having molecular weights identical to within one standard deviation of molecular weights of proteins of said starting material, as determined by SDS-PAGE electrophoresis with Cooomassie staining. 50. The moringa seed composition according to any one of claims 35 - 44, produced according to the method of any one of claims 1 - 34. 51. The moringa seed composition according to claim 45, produced according to the method of any one of claims 1 - 34. 52. The moringa seed composition according to claim 46, produced according to the method of any one of claims 1 - 34. 53. The moringa seed composition according to claim 47, produced according to the method of any one of claims 1 - 34. 54. The moringa seed composition according to claim 48, produced according to the method of any one of claims 1 - 34. 55. The moringa seed composition according to claim 49, produced according to the method of any one of claims 1 - 34.
